supporting_sources.title: Why AI Actually Won't Take Your Job

Luma just launched its new image model, Uni-1. I had early access and have been using it for the last few days. ItвЂ™s extremely powerful. It works really well with text and reference images. But what impressed me most is its text-to-image quality. I ran some of my best prompts, and it nailed almost every single one. Here are a few examples 1/2 Reference image outputs

source_materials.title: Why AI Actually Won't Take Your Job
source_materials.excerpt: ## Why AI Actually Won't Take Your Job The AI Daily Brief: Artificial Intelligence News and Analysis Mar 22, 2026 00:00 32:51 #### The Coming AI Rules Battle AI is rising faster than any other issue in American political polling, and the White House just dropped a legislative framework that's already drawing fire from both sides вЂ” populist right critics calling AI "profoundly anti-human" and Democrats saying voluntary standards won't cut it. The real question is whether this four-page opening move can hold the center as midterms approach and public anxiety about jobs keeps climbing. In the headlines: OpenAI plans to double its workforce with a major enterprise push, FedEx is training all 400,000 employees on AI, and Meta's internal agents are now talking to each other.For all the links referenced in the show, sign up for the newsletter: вЃ вЃ вЃ https://aidailybrief.beehiiv.com/вЃ вЃ вЃ Brought to you by:KPMG вЂ“ Agentic AI is powering a potential $3 trillion productivity shift, and KPMGвЂ™s new paper, Agentic AI Untangled, gives leaders a clear framework to decide whether to build, buy, or borrowвЂ”download it at вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ www.kpmg.us/NavigateвЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Mercury - Modern banking for business and now personal accounts. source_materials.title: Luma just launched its new image model, Uni-1. I had early access and have been using it for the last few days. ItвЂ™s extremely powerful. It works really well with text and reference images. But what impressed me most is its text-to-image quality. I ran some of my best prompts, and it nailed almost every single one. Here are a few examples 1/2 Reference image outputs
